root_text.an7.33:1.1: “Imaṁ, bhikkhave, rattiṁ aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho, bhikkhave, sā devatā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an7.33:1.4: Satthugāravatā, dhammagāravatā, saṅghagāravatā, sikkhāgāravatā, samādhigāravatā, hirigāravatā, ottappagāravatā.
root_text.an7.33:1.5: Ime kho, bhante, satta dhammā bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattantī’ti.
root_text.an7.33:1.7: Idaṁ vatvā maṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.an7.33:1.1: “Mendicants, tonight, a glorious deity, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, came to me, bowed, stood to one side, and said to me:
translation_text.an7.33:1.2: ‘Sir, these seven things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.
translation_text.an7.33:1.4: Respect for the Teacher, for the teaching, for the Saṅgha, for the training, for immersion, for conscience, and for prudence.
translation_text.an7.33:1.5: These seven things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.’
translation_text.an7.33:1.6: That is what that deity said.
translation_text.an7.33:1.7: Then he bowed and respectfully circled me, keeping me on his right side, before vanishing right there.
translation_text.an7.33:2.1: One respectful of the Teacher and the teaching,
translation_text.an7.33:3.3: it is impossible for them to decline;
translation_text.an7.33:3.4: they have drawn near to extinguishment.”
